claim: Daemon IPC bridge bind is present, un-gated, and runs after server.connect in BOTH running dist artifacts; rules out (a) regression-removed bind and (b) conditional gate.

Socket inodes were created at daemon start (file exists, srw------- 0o600, mtime 18:35) and the daemons are ALIVE (pid 60718 code-index, pid 61317 advisor) but NO process holds the socket FD; only spec-memory pid 61626 holds its socket. Rules out crash-during-bind / pure silent ENOENT.

close() unlinks the socket file (socket-server.ts:282-291) and the EADDRINUSE path unlinks+rebinds (socket-server.ts:242-253); a transient bind->close or racing re-create leaves a stale/orphaned inode with an old mtime and no listener, matching F2.

The owner-lease heartbeat keeps the daemon looking healthy independent of bridge-socket liveness; structural asymmetry vs working spec-memory is the idle-monitor + lease teardown ordering, not the bind call. spec-memory survives because a long-lived stdio client keeps markActivity fresh.
socket-server.ts has drifted across all three copies (012 D-001 accepted-risk realized); working and failing copies are no longer identical, so per-copy bind/teardown behavior can differ.

note: Concurrency context matches strategy: code-index daemon 60718, advisor 61317, spec-memory child 61626 all started in the same 18:35 trio recycle; N-way secondary launchers (2 Claude + 4 codex) churn the bridge.
Packet 012 verification only ever covered single-primary bind + one-secondary attach; N-way steady-state socket survival was never directly verified (the SC-004 caveat).
chmod 0o600 on the socket runs only after a successful listenOnce, so a 0o600 socket file proves the bind reached 'listening' at least once.

Launchers execute the dist artifacts (mcp_server/dist/index.js; dist/mcp_server/advisor-server.js), so dist is the source of runtime truth; source/dist drift on the bind was checked and is absent (index.ts:149 == dist/index.js:127).
Idle timeout default 30 min; daemons are ~73 min old yet did not exit, so stdin activity (daemon-owner Claude) kept them alive while the bridge socket was independently lost.
(a) later edit removed/broke the bind — bind present in source AND running dist.
(b) conditional gate (SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_BRIDGE_DISABLED) — no gate around the bind call in either dist (0 grep hits).
daemon crashed/exited during bind — both daemons alive and lease-heartbeating.
un-rebuilt dist (source/dist drift on the bind) — source bind == running dist bind.
